intro.title: After the closure
intro.narrator: Some days smell tiredness, pepper and things we didn't have time to say.
intro.text:
You're closing the shop.

The neon above the window flashes one last time.

*Horlogerie Verneuil — Repairs & Restorations*

You put your tools in their velvet box.
Little pliers.
Wolves.
Thin springs like hair.

Outside, the city is running.
She's still running.

You spend the night fixing watches that nobody watches anymore.

Sometimes you wonder if time really exists.
Or if people just pretend.

You're eating a warm-up blanquette in the kitchen.

She's good anyway.

You sleep on the couch.

hub_start.title: The Establishment of Songs
hub_start.narrator: In dreams, even counters can drift.
hub_start.text:
You open your eyes behind your establishment.

But the shop isn't exactly the same anymore.

Watches on shelves turn backwards.
The hands turn around the dial in seconds.
Or in hours. Hard to say.

A layer appears on the set, brilliant as a precision tool.

Three doors appear in the back wall.

The city roars behind each one.

choices.text: Go through the clock door with your bag
Observe the cat of the dream

chat_hub.title: The Chat du Comptoir
chat_hub.narrator: Some guides prefer to sleep on the answers.
chat_hub.text:
The cat is lying on the stable.

He opens an eye when you approach.

Around him, unassembled watches whose wheels continue to run on their own.

**"You fix time. *

He doesn't really talk.
But you still understand.

**"Then you should know that this one is broken. *

choices.text: Ask him to show you the way

ville_intro.title: The City of Court
ville_intro.text:
You're coming to a town where everyone runs.

Passersby eat while walking.
The kids do their homework on the subway.
People consult their watch but do not look at the time — just the movement of the needles.

As if checking was enough.
Like time was better when we watched him.

You're taking out your repair magnifier.

The watches you see are all set at the same time.
Not the right one.

choices.text: Go talk to the old lady
you feel a delicious smell coming out of your bag, you can install a table and serve the blanquette

vieille_dame.title: The Lady of the Bench
vieille_dame.narrator: Sometimes someone waits less for an answer than a presence.
vieille_dame.text:
You sit next to her.

She doesn't seem surprised.

**"Everyone is running. *

You've been asking her how long.

**"Since it was decided that time lost was a mistake. *

She takes an old watch out of her pocket.
The dial is cracked.
The needles are stopped.

*"My husband wore it. He never ran. *

She puts the watch back in her pocket.

**"She stopped the day he stopped running too. *

rue_sans_pause.title: Rue Sans Pause
rue_sans_pause.narrator: A city without a break ends up forgetting why it is moving forward.
rue_sans_pause.text:
You're watching passersby.

They're all running in the same direction.

But at the end of the street, there's a wall.

They turn right.
Run again.
Turn left.
Run again.

A circuit.

You look at their wrist.
Each watch displays a different time.
But they're all moving too fast.

*Someone pulled up all the springs too hard. *

You know this problem.

choices.text: Install the large table in the middle of the avenue

etudiant_presse.title: Student Stressed
etudiant_presse.narrator: Success sometimes takes the form of a pile of leaves that flies away.
etudiant_presse.text:
The student picks up his leaves by shaking.

**"I'm late. *

A leaf flies away.
She's wearing an exam date.

But the date is tomorrow.

**"You're not late. *

He looks at you like you're speaking a foreign language.

**"If I don't run, I'm late. *

He's running back.

You're watching your watch.
Yours is moving at the right speed.

That's why she looks broken to everyone.

metro.title: Metro Sans Stop
metro.narrator: Some trains don't drive anywhere, but everyone gets there anyway.
metro.text:
The subway has been driving for a long time.

People look at the ground.

A man sleeps up, a briefcase tight against his chest.
A woman responds to messages of an absent air.
A teenager listens to music with his eyes closed — he is the only one who seems to be breathing.

You look at the signs.

Stations scroll too fast to be read.

You're asking someone what time the next stop comes.

He's watching his watch.

**"In exactly enough time not to be late. *

choices.text: Stay in the subway to see the next stop
Exit at the next imaginary stop

restaurant_express.title: Express Restaurant
restaurant_express.narrator: Here, the dishes arrive before hunger.
restaurant_express.text:
You're entering a tiny restaurant.

The waiter will give you the check before you even sit down.

The dishes are already on the table.
Already half eaten.
Already cooled.

**"Quick service is our specialty. *

You ask if you can order a blanquette.

He's looking at you.

**"It takes three hours. *

Like it's an insult.

**" Exactly. *

choices.text: Open your bag and serve something real

table_ville.title: The Slowing Table
table_ville.narrator: The first act of resistance is sometimes to lay a tablecloth.
table_ville.text:
You set up a small table in the middle of the sidewalk.

That's absurd.

So, of course, it attracts a few eyes.

You put on a blanquette you had in a box.
Drinks.
Bread.

Passers-by slow down.

One stops.

**"What is it? *

**"A meal. *

**"How long does it take? *

**"The time it takes. *

He's sitting.

choices.text: Move forward with the shiny ladle

fin_pas_le_temps.title: Not Time
fin_pas_le_temps.narrator: If we get there soon, we sometimes forget where we were going.
fin_pas_le_temps.text:
You start running.

At first, it's to follow the others.
Then not to bother.
Then because everyone runs.

Your steps are accelerating.

The ladle falls somewhere behind you.

You don't stop to pick it up.

Your watch is moving faster and faster.

The needles rotate so fast that they seem motionless.

Somewhere, a shop closes.

The neon goes out.

fin_retard.title: Delay
fin_retard.narrator: The delay is a loop with new shoes.
fin_retard.text:
You run with the student.

You turn left.

Then right.

Then left again.

The streets are repeated.

The screens are repeated.

Passersby repeat themselves.

After an impossible time to measure, you finally arrive.

In front of you:

the same avenue.

Same bench.

Same old lady.

The same student who picks up his leaves.

He's looking at you.

**"I'm late. *

And you understand that you are, too.

fin_metro.title: The Last Judgment
fin_metro.narrator: Some journeys are only long because you dare not go down.
fin_metro.text:
The subway continues.

The lights are flashing.

The voice announced:

> Next stop:
> Your retirement.

Nobody's moving.

The standing man always sleeps.

Her watch shows the same time from two stations.

You look at yours.

She stopped, too.

You didn't notice.

hub_admin.title: The Second Gate
hub_admin.narrator: Dreams don't let you go with one good action.
hub_admin.text:
You come back behind your floating counter.

The shiny ladle rests near the crate.

The clock door closed slowly.

The city that ran finally breathes.

But the other two doors stay here.

The morning door still doesn't appear.

The cat jumps on the counter, pushes the sausage from the end of the leg, then looks at the door covered with forms.

On the notebook, a new sentence reads:

> We can't wake up until the papers dream.

You understand.

The dream is not over.

choices.text: Pass the administrative door with your bag

admin_intro.title: The Forms Office
admin_intro.narrator: Here, the corridors are straight, but nobody gets to the end.
admin_intro.text:
You're arriving in a grey building.

Mountains of files go up to the ceiling.

Clocks everywhere.
All at different times.
All fake.

A man gives you a form.

**"What for? *

**"To be able to request the form of the reason for your arrival. *

You look at the clocks.

They're moving forward.
But not in the right direction.

I could settle them, you think.

It's not the question here, you understand.

ticket_admin.title: Ticket A38
ticket_admin.narrator: Waiting becomes stranger when no one knows why.
ticket_admin.text:
You're taking a ticket.

It displays:

> A38

The light panel displays:

> A2

A woman has been waiting here for so long that she brought a plant.

The plant itself took a ticket.

You look at the student.

It has been filling the same line for several minutes.

Name:
Reason for Name:
Justification:

His hands are shaking.

The cat passes between the chairs and sleeps on the A3 ticket.

Nobody knows if the line can move on.

choices.text: Take something out of your bag and share it

ascenseur_admin.title: The Administrative Ascensor
ascenseur_admin.narrator: Even going up a floor sometimes requires a complete folder.
ascenseur_admin.text:
The elevator is at the end of the hall.

A metallic voice announces:

> Please provide proof of climb.

You press the button.

The vote resumed:

> Please provide proof that you have the right to support.

The cat sleeps in front of the buttons.

He's rubbing.

The elevator won't ask for anything anymore.

A faceless employee whispers:

**"It's been blocking the system since this morning. It's the quietest day we've had in years. *

choices.text: Enjoy the calm to organize a lunch break

trombones.title: The Trombone Man
trombones.narrator: Some tasks survive long after they have been forgotten.
trombones.text:
A man classifies trombones by color.

Red.
Blue.
Grey.
A grey a little greyer than the other gray.

You ask him if it's urgent.

He looks at you with the eyes of someone who hasn't heard the word "urgent" in so long that he doesn't know what it means anymore.

**"This is my job. *

**"And before? *

He's packing a trombone.

**"I would pay watches. *

choices.text: Tell him some things are shared instead of classifying themselves

pause_dej.title: Wheels, Bread and Tampons
pause_dej.narrator: An office becomes less terrible when someone breaks bread.
pause_dej.text:
You put the bread and the sausage on the counter.

A giant buffer stops in the middle of the flight.

Employees look up.

How long have they not eaten something real?

One of them holds out his hand to the bread.

Another one hesitates.

As if taking time to eat was an unfilled form.

choices.text: Store an unnecessary buffer in your bag

admin_resolution.title: The Needless Stamp
admin_resolution.narrator: Sometimes the best tools are the ones you decide not to use anymore.
admin_resolution.text:
You get a little tampon on the counter.

It bears a strange mention:

> PASS — Validated on an exceptional basis

You put it on your own form.

Nobody's protesting.

Maybe because no one knows exactly which stamps are official.

Or maybe because you look like someone who knows what he's doing with small precision tools.

fin_formulaire.title: The Final Form
fin_formulaire.narrator: Some traps start with a check box.
fin_formulaire.text:
You take the form.

He only has one page.

Then two.

Then twenty.

They ask you for your name, then the reason for your name, then a confirmation that you have a reason.

You sit down to fill more comfortably.

When you look up, you're behind a desk.

In front of you, someone's waiting.

You want to help him.

But a sentence comes out of your mouth before you can stop it:

**"One signature is missing. *

A tampon appears in your hand.

Again.

Again.

Again.

fin_employe.title: Monthly employee
fin_employe.narrator: Some promotions look like traps with an ergonomic chair.
fin_employe.text:
You wait.

The panel changes from A2 to A2 bis.

Then A2 additional.

Then A2 final version.

After a while, an employee gives you a badge.

> MONTH EMPLOYEE.

You're trying to explain that you're not employed here.

He's smiling at you.

**"That's what all the new people say. *

Your office appears.

Your chair appears.

Your installation form appears.

A note is placed on it:

> Your lunch break is scheduled in 17 years.

hub_market.title: The Third Gate
hub_market.narrator: One good thing sometimes calls for another.
hub_market.text:
You come back behind your floating counter.

The shiny ladle rests near the crate.
The useless buffer is now used as a clipboard.

Two doors closed.

The city is slowing down.
The administration is breathing.

But the neon of the third door still flashes.

The cat gets on the counter.

He sniffs the air, looks at you, then looks at the neon door.

On the notebook, a sentence appears:

> Some get lost running.
> Others get lost buying what they didn't need.

The morning door is still not there.

There's still a world to go through.

choices.text: Go through the neon door with your bag

market_intro.title: The Infinite Supermarket
market_intro.narrator: Here, everything is available. Except the exit.
market_intro.text:
You're coming to a huge supermarket.

The neons are buzzing.

The rays repeat infinitely in mirrors.
The radius of disposable watches makes seven whole alleys.

*Waterproof up to 50 meters. *
* One-year non-renewable guarantee. *
* Accuracy: ±5 minutes per month. *

You're looking at one of them.

She's already four minutes late.

She just got out of the box.

choices.text: Talking to the client with the overflowing cart
Install a table in the middle of the radius

cliente_chariot.title: The Cart too full
cliente_chariot.narrator: Some baskets are full of things we didn't really want.
cliente_chariot.text:
The client pushes a huge cart.

It overflows with identical boxes.

You ask what she buys.

**"Time savings. *

She shows the boxes.

* 12 minutes a day. *
* More effective. *
* Family format. *

**"And with this time saved, what do you do? *

It stops.

Really think.

**"I'm looking for ways to earn more. *

conserves.title: The Ray of the Conserves
conserves.narrator: When everything is preserved, even hesitations become long.
conserves.text:
The old lady reads the ingredients of a box of peas.

She's resting her.

Take another one.

Rest.

**"Looking for something specific? *

**"I'm looking for peas that have grown somewhere. *

She shows the label.

*Origin: Product in optimized area. *

**"Before, I knew where I came from. *

You understand.

That's how you feel about serial watches.

choices.text: Open the can and serve with the chicken
Compare all boxes one by one

caisses_auto.title: Automatic Caisses
caisses_auto.narrator: Some machines know very well how to say no.
caisses_auto.text:
The automatic body repeats:

> Unexpected item in the packing area.

You put an old pocket watch on the carpet.

> Unexpected item in the packing area.

**"I know. *

You take it back.

The machine doesn't know what to do with an object that lasts.

choices.text: Unplug the box and invite the line to eat

table_market.title: The kitchen too big
table_market.narrator: When people remember they're hungry, the rays stop being infinite.
table_market.text:
You put the roast chicken on a small table.

Nobody knows where the table comes from.

Clients are approaching.
Some forgot you could eat without a screen in front of you.

A child asks what it is.

**"A meal. *

**"How long does it take? *

**"The right one. *

He sits on the floor.

It's a good start.

choices.text: Moving forward with the little neon light

fin_carte.title: Loyalty Card
fin_carte.narrator: Some rewards cost more than purchases.
fin_carte.text:
You follow promotions.

A loyalty card appears in your hand.

Then a second.

Then a third.

Each card promises more benefits than the previous one.

One voice announced:

> Congratulations, you are now a priority customer.

The rays are opening in front of you.

Your cart is growing.

Your pockets fill with tickets.

You don't know what you wanted to buy anymore.

But you won 3% on something.

Later, much later, you sleep in the bedding department.

The car wishes you a good night.

fin_bug.title: Article Unexpected
fin_bug.narrator: Maybe the bug was the only thing that was still sincere.
fin_bug.text:
You keep scanning.

BIP.

> Unexpected item in the packing area.

BIP.

> Unexpected item in the packing area.

BIP.

> Unexpected item in the packing area.

The world is shaking.

Neon flashes.

Customers freeze.

The cat is watching you.

He seems almost disappointed.

The caisse takes over:

> Unexpected customer in the existence zone.

Then the whole world freeze.

fin_rayon_12.title: Section 12
fin_rayon_12.narrator: All outings look like promotions when you don't know what you're looking for.
fin_rayon_12.text:
You compare the boxes.

Extra fine peas.
Fine peas.
Almost sincere.
Limited edition peas.
Peas with promise of happiness.

The rays are repeated.

Your basket is getting heavier.

You don't remember taking anything.

There's a jingle in the speakers.

**"Exceptional offer. Exceptional offer. Exceptional offer. *

You're passing the 12th.

Then again.

Then again.

After a while, you understand.

You're not looking for the exit anymore.

You're only looking for the next ray.

final_hub.title: The Calmed Counter
final_hub.narrator: The dream keeps track of what we warmed up.
final_hub.text:
You come back behind your floating establishment.

This time, everything is different.

Watches on shelves turn at the right speed.
Not too fast.
Not too slow.

The blanquette is hot.

Outside, a few people slowed down.
Not much.
Just enough to notice that the sky exists.

banquet.title: The Banquet of Ordinary Worlds
banquet.location: REVE — A BIG TABLE BETWEEN THE WORLDS
banquet.narrator: The colder worlds sometimes repair themselves around a table too large.
banquet.text:
You're serving people in a hurry.

You fix a watch for the waiting employees.
You cut the sausage for the supermarket people.
You offer bread to the old lady on the bench.

No one runs during this meal.

It's not much.

Just a meal.
Just a few people sitting.
Just a watchmaker who knows that some things can't be accelerated.

reveil.title: Morning Opens
reveil.narrator: Some doors open before you even touch the handle.
reveil.text:
You're slowly opening your eyes.

The morning light crosses the shop window.

For a few seconds,
Don't move.

You hear:

- The noise of the needles.
- The noise of the city that's starting again.
- The sound of your own breath.

You're up.

You put a bottle of wine in the fridge for tonight.

The blanquette's been simmering since yesterday.

You open the shop.

*Horlogerie Verneuil — Repairs & Restorations*

The neon flashes once.

Then light up.
